But the bottom line is this, when you strip away all the crap and all the self-justifying and self-storytelling and any ridiculous reason you've conjured up in your own mind to try to convince yourself that you're settling for anything but the absolute best you could possibly be every day of your life is not even slightly okay. When you strip all that nonsense away, you simply can't deny that extraordinary standards is the gift that every man or every woman gives to him or herself. It's the gift that no one else can ever give you and no one else could ever take away. Listen, people respect people. No, they admire people who have high standards and they revere them. They want to do business with people like that. They say that people respect people who act like they know what they're talking about, like act like experts and in fact, who are experts. You can be an expert at the age of 22 years old, age doesn't matter. You can also have extraordinary standards at 22. You know, age doesn't matter for that either. At 62 years old, you could have low standards or high standards. Your age is the only limit you have to send your mind. And the standard you set for yourself is the insurance policy to make sure that your life doesn't pass you by in a state of mediocrity and averageism. You know, I keep hearing that again and again, that people all over the world, my age, my age, you know, I'm too old, I'm too young, I'm what, and they use it as an excuse and it's all crap. It's a story, a reason. It doesn't matter. The so-called old boy work, if there actually is one in your industry, there's nothing this network loves more than to watch a young, hardworking person coming right out of the house, looking sharp and acting sharp and someone with high standards at every level and a successful businessman can sniff that out a mile away. They'll actually do business with you first if they respect your standards and you approach them with conviction. Listen, we haven't met in person yet, but the way you've committed yourself to mastering these modules, it tells you that you and I are at least on some level kindred spirits. And if there's one thing I could promise you, it's that you're capable of achieving absolute greatness in any aspect of your life. But you're going to have to work really, really hard to get it. That's it. You know, you have to learn every single you need right now and you have to take what you've learned here and really go out there into the field without delay and start taking some serious action and kicking some serious financial butt here. And you all to yourself, if you're not doing that, you're shorting only yourself. You know, life is short. You know how old I am? I'm almost 50. I've lived half my life already. You know, someone say I probably live 10 lives actually or at least a bunch of lives, right? But you know what? I swear this to many times when I get that little bit of pang and nervousness that my own life is passing me by. I lived half of it already, more than half of it most likely. I mean, how many more years do I really have left? How many months do I have left? Who really knows? You know, but whenever I find myself questioning, you know, should I go for something or should I not? Those things, should I work at something? Should I take a chance? I say, you know, damn straight I will. You know, I don't know how long I have left. None of us do. Every year that we have on this earth is a gift. And to go out there and take action and pursue your hopes, your dreams, your vision, you know, everything that's out there for you, it's a gift that you give to yourself. If you're out there just thinking and acting from a place of fear, you're being held back by a thousand and one limiting beliefs and you're holding yourself to some lousy, disempowering standard, okay? You're not living your life at all. You're already dead. You're not a positive example to your family, to the people that you love, to the community that you live in, to the very planet you walk on. You're not being the best that you can be and you owe it to yourself to be that. You know, listen, if nothing else, you owe it to yourself and you owe it to your children if you have any. To set an example to the people around you that living life truly at the highest level is what life is all about. And it's not about haunting behind fear and uncertainty and mediocrity. You know, listen, you know, average sucks. You hear that? Average sucks. You know what the enemy of great is? It's good. Good is the enemy of great. See, you feel good so there's not enough pain so you don't do anything about it to actually get great results. Good sucks. Average sucks. The beauty of life. The Quan, the beauty, the passion, the connection, the love, the respect, the community and the money. That's what extraordinary standards are all about. Not just finding your Quan but actually living your Quan. Does it take some extra work into building? Yeah, you know what it does. Some mental fortitude, yeah. Some determination, you bet it does. Some strategic preparation, it takes that too. Do you need to become reasonably proficient with the straight life system? Yeah. And it will take a bit of time? Yeah. To burn bright in your life to be the best you can possibly be and live your Quan, that's what life is all about. Everything else is just a mere whiff of life. It's not the full flavor of it. To actually go out there and grasp everything that life has to offer you to become literally a top producer and to enjoy all the fruits, all the respect that comes along with that, that's what you truly capable of. Everything else is just your story. Your capable of absolute greatness and don't you ever, ever forget that. And I'll see you in the next session.
